blurb:
This place on Franklin Gateway is one of those spots that feels like a local secret even though it has over five thousand reviews. It's casual and low-key, and the prices are very reasonable for how good the food is. I'd call it a solid weeknight dinner or a quick lunch stop when you're on that side of town.

The shawarma is what most people go for, and it's the real deal. The falafel is great too, and they have solid veggie options if you've got someone in your group who doesn't eat meat. Save room for one of their desserts if you can.

Open every day from 10am to 9pm, so it's pretty easy to fit in.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
If you've lived around here for any amount of time, you already know about Marietta Diner. It's open 24 hours, seven days a week, and that alone makes it worth having in your back pocket. The neon sign on Cobb Parkway is basically a local landmark at this point. Good for late nights, early breakfasts, or whenever you just need a big comfortable meal without any fuss.

The menu is enormous, and they do Greek specialties alongside all the classic diner staples. The breakfast plates are generous and the kind of thing that actually fills you up. It's hard to go wrong here, honestly.

Parking is easy and the place moves fast even when it's busy. Great option when you've got a group with different tastes or you just don't want to think too hard about where to go.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
Douceur De France is a French bakery on South Marietta Parkway and it has a near-perfect rating for good reason. It's bright, it smells amazing when you walk in, and the baked goods are the real thing. My wife and I stopped in on a Saturday morning and we both agreed we should have been coming here for years.

The baguettes are great, and the quiches make for a really solid light lunch. The petits fours are worth picking up if you're bringing something to a gathering. It's the kind of place you'd use for a slow weekend brunch or a nice weekday breakfast before work.

Just note that on Sundays they close at 3:30pm, so don't plan a late visit that day.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA

blurb:
Marietta Square Market is a food hall right off the Square with around twenty different vendors under one roof. It's the easiest solution when your family can't agree on what to eat, because everyone can just go grab something different and meet back at a table. We've ended up here more times than I can count after walking around the Square on a weekend.

The lineup rotates and covers a lot of ground, so there's usually something for everyone whether you're in the mood for something light or something more substantial. It has a nice indoor vibe and doesn't feel rushed.

Friday and Saturday they stay open until 10pm. Good spot to keep in mind when you're already spending time around the Square.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
Rio is a Brazilian all-you-can-eat place off Powers Ferry, and it's genuinely fun if you've never done that style of dining before. The idea is simple: they keep bringing food to your table and you eat until you tap out. It's the kind of place that works really well for a group celebration or a night when you just want to go all in.

The barbecue is the main event, but don't skip the cheese bread. It's warm and a little addictive. They also do feijoada stew, which is a hearty Brazilian classic worth trying. They're open for breakfast too, which is a little unexpected but apparently pretty popular.

Friday and Saturday they stay open until 11pm, which makes it a decent option for a later dinner on the weekend.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
